valorant.last_update_card:
## Valorant âš¡ Patch 12.05

### TL;DR
Act 2 launches with new Controller Miks reshaping the support meta, while Yoru and Clove â€” the two most dominant flex picks of Act 1 â€” both get gutted simultaneously. The duelist meta just got a hard reset and Controller players are eating well.

### What Got Buffed
**Skye â€” Guiding Light (Flashes)**
Flashes are now rechargeable on a 60-second cooldown instead of consuming limited charges. If you play deep and survive into late-round, you can stack multiple flashes back up. Initiator players who actually stay alive get rewarded.
Verdict: **Play this now**

**Miks (New Agent â€” Controller)**
Sound-based kit: dual-use grenade (heals allies or concusses enemies), smokes, Combat Stim that refreshes on kills shared with a teammate, and an ultimate sound wave that knocks back + deafens + slows an entire push. Purpose-built for coordinated team play â€” the best support Controller added in years if the stim-on-kill loop is as strong as it reads.
Verdict: **Worth trying** â€” ceiling is very high but requires a partner who communicates

**Lotus (Map Returns)**
Attacker-side wall pen removed off barrier drop, jump to Vines repositioned to help attackers escape the lobby without eating utility dumps, Tree Room wall reinforced. Net result: attacking A-site is less miserable and wall-bang cheese is gone.
Verdict: **Situational** â€” map is playable again

### What Got Nerfed
**Yoru**
Gatecrash beacon duration cut from 30s â†’ 15s. Blindside charges reduced from 2 â†’ 1. The 30-second decoy lurk window that made him borderline broken is gone. One flash means you get one real play per round â€” no more double-flash fakeout entries.
How dominant before: S-tier pick in pro + high-elo ranked, bleeding into Initiator/Duelist hybrid value
Verdict: **Still playable** â€” the fundamental kit still works; he just can't solo-carry info anymore

**Clove**
Ruse post-death duration: 14s â†’ 6s. Meddle AoE: 6m â†’ 4m. The "free posthumous Controller value" that made Clove so frustrating is effectively halved. 6 seconds of smoke after death barely buys a push; the old 14 seconds was a full round swing.
How dominant before: Pick-or-ban tier, required no coordination to generate value
Verdict: **Shelve it** â€” the entire design proposition (die and still win rounds) is significantly weaker

### What's OP Right Now
**Miks + any aggro Initiator (Skye, KAY/O)** â€” Stim-share into flash-clear is the new fastest entry package. Miks buffs the Initiator doing the work; Skye now has recharging flashes to keep the pressure going. This combo will get solved in pro play first but it's already broken in ranked.

**Omen/Viper** â€” With Clove nerfed, the other two dominant solo-Controller picks fill the vacuum. Omen's one-way smokes on the reworked Lotus A-site are genuinely oppressive now that wall pens are removed.

**Classic + Frenzy pistol rounds** â€” Knockout mode aside, the meta shift means more aggressive early round dueling while teams figure out the new controller landscape. Sidearm mastery matters more this week.

### What's Dead
**Clove at any rank below Diamond** â€” Most players weren't extracting value from the post-death smoke anyway; now the window is so short it requires exact timing. Drop rate in ranked will be steep.

**Yoru soloqueue** â€” Two charges of Blindside covered for bad coordination. One charge does not. He remains viable in coordinated 5-stack but is effectively off the ranked table for most players.

**Abyss and Corrode** â€” Both maps removed from competitive rotation. Any map-specific strats you had are irrelevant until they return.

### Under the Radar
**Skye's recharge system fundamentally changes her role.** Previously she burned through flashes early and went passive. Now a Skye who plays deep and gets value can cycle back into a second or third flash within the same round. This rewards aggressive Skye players and punishes teams that assume she's "out of flashes" â€” a psychological advantage that most opponents won't adjust to for at least two weeks.

### Meta Verdict
Patch 12.05 is a genuine shake-up: the two safest picks from Act 1 (Clove and Yoru) both got hit hard in the same patch, Lotus is back with structural fixes that make it more attacker-favored, and a new Controller with a high-skill ceiling just entered the pool. The meta this week belongs to Controller players â€” Omen and Viper move from "solid" to "dominant by default" while Miks gets figured out. On the Duelist side, Jett and Reyna are your best bets; Yoru requires a team built around him now. Play Controller, learn Lotus A-site fast, and if you're running Initiator, lock Skye and do not waste flashes early.
